sections.label: Technique
sections.placeholder: Multidetector CT of the pulmonary arteries acquired after intravenous iodinated contrast. Multiplanar reformats reviewed. State kVp/contrast volume/phase and DLP as per local dose policy.

sections.label: Comparison
sections.placeholder: Date and type of relevant prior imaging, if available.

sections.label: Findings
sections.placeholder:
Pulmonary arteries — central to subsegmental (filling defects; most proximal occluded level [saddle/main / lobar / segmental / subsegmental]; laterality; acute vs chronic/organised): 
Clot burden (uni- vs bilateral, central vs peripheral): 
Right-heart strain (RV:LV short-axis ratio with >~1.0 as the threshold for RV dilatation, septal bowing/flattening, IVC/hepatic-vein contrast reflux, main PA calibre): 
Lungs (pulmonary infarct, effusion): 
Mediastinum / incidental:

sections.label: Impression
sections.placeholder: Concise, prioritised bulleted impression. Lead with presence/absence of acute PE, its most proximal level and laterality, then a right-heart strain line (RV:LV ratio relative to ~1.0, septal bowing, reflux); escalate via the critical-result pathway for central clot or strain, and close with approved follow-up phrasing only.

sections.label: Recommendations
sections.placeholder: Evidence-based follow-up or correlation, drawn from approved phrasing, only if indicated.
